messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: As they have only given you the 3mf (stl) you cannot actually edit their design in CAD. Instead you can patch over it in the slicer with another object containing the logo you want. This "patch" could be distributed separately and not cause issues with the licence, you just need to align it correctly before slicing. (edited)
messages.content_html: As they have only given you the 3mf (stl) you cannot actually edit their design in CAD. Instead you can patch over it in the slicer with another object containing the logo you want. This "patch" could be distributed separately and not cause issues with the licence, you just need to align it correctly before slicing. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

Meshtastic RAK19003 Wisblock Mini Slim case by Badger MakerWorld: D...
I needed a slim case for a Meshtastic mesh node that I could easily carry in my pocket. I was tired of bulky nodes and wanted something smaller.The Reg case squeezes in a 103450 lipo battery and the RAK19003 board. The Nano case takes a 102535 battery. I have options for an external antenna or a case that uses the included small antenna for a mo...

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: Nice, does the top surface just sparkle or is it a special rough finish, and is it printed in an enclosure? I use PETG-CF which would look similar but is probably easier to print. Cannot see the advantage of nylon over petg, especially with cf, I was led to believe it was better for tools. (edited)
messages.content_html: Nice, does the top surface just sparkle or is it a special rough finish, and is it printed in an enclosure? I use PETG-CF which would look similar but is probably easier to print. Cannot see the advantage of nylon over petg, especially with cf, I was led to believe it was better for tools. (edited)

messages.text: Get some PETG-CF. I have an Ender3v2 upgraded to S1Pro spec, but I printed plenty whie it was stock that are is still in use. (edited)

messages.author: Quark
messages.text: I'm working on a original case for the t114 node. Powered by one 18650. Might need some finetuning but its a first test print. Made a make shift logo and it is printing as we speak.... I hate it when people just publish stl files instead of stepfiles :p So I designed one from scratch and adjust an redesign as needed.
messages.content_html: I'm working on a original case for the t114 node. Powered by one 18650. Might need some finetuning but its a first test print. Made a make shift logo and it is printing as we speak.... I hate it when people just publish stl files instead of stepfiles :p So I designed one from scratch and adjust an redesign as needed.

messages.author: EvilBlueSmartie
messages.text: Hi all. I just got my first Heltec and decided to put together my own enclosure for it. This is intended as a desktop unit to sit on my desk at work as a repeater. Fits a Heltec v3 and a 3000 mAh (104060) battery, just in case there's a power cut. https://makerworld.com/en/models/2442720-heltec-desktop-enclosure

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: If you want to get really fancy and dont have a multi material printer you can print little letters that slot in to the recessed area, or manually change colours if your slicer supports it. If you use two different materials such as high temp Nylon/PA or PETG with additives and make the letters PLA you can even try to melt them in places. A good way to waste several hours of your life
messages.content_html: If you want to get really fancy and dont have a multi material printer you can print little letters that slot in to the recessed area, or manually change colours if your slicer supports it. If you use two different materials such as high temp Nylon/PA or PETG with additives and make the letters PLA you can even try to melt them in places. A good way to waste several hours of your life

messages.text: I designed a Repeater for ~50€ based on the RAK19003/RAK19007 and would like to get some feedback from this amazing community. Enjoy https://makerworld.com/de/models/2397587-meshcore-meshtastic-repeater

messages.author: Tumezie
messages.text: I printed this in pla as a prototype. I'd like to be able to do a few things, 1 - allow for solar panel size modification, so I ncan use a panel I already have 2 - option for 18650 batteries ( just one or two. 3 - the N connection hole was too small for the N to IPX connectors I have. 4 - again making it bigger but allowing a 19007 board (temp and humidity sensors are handy) Overall tho it's a beautiful design, nice and small and looks to all fit together very well. Awesome work 🫡
messages.content_html: I printed this in pla as a prototype. Awesome work 🫡

messages.author: Bino
messages.text: 1 - This is possible in the customizer, check for the solarpanel width/depth/height 2 - This is already in the workings 3 - Which size was to tough? Can you provide some measurements? Could it be, that the filament is not calibrated since you switched from ASA to PLA? 4 - In the customizer you can adjust the size of the housing. These is also a GSP model switch, which increases the gap underneath the board. Though you can add any sensors you like
messages.content_html: 1 - This is possible in the customizer, check for the solarpanel width/depth/height 2 - This is already in the workings 3 - Which size was to tough? Can you provide some measurements? Could it be, that the filament is not calibrated since you switched from ASA to PLA? 4 - In the customizer you can adjust the size of the housing. These is also a GSP model switch, which increases the gap underneath the board. Though you can add any sensors you like

messages.reply_html:
Bino
1 - This is possible in the customizer, check for the solarpanel width/depth/height 2 - This is already in the workings 3 - Which size was to tough? Can you provide some measurements? Could it be, that the filament is not calibrated since you switched from ASA to PLA? 4 - In the customizer you can adjust the size of the housing. These is also a GSP model switch, which increases the gap underneath the board. Though you can add any sensors you like

messages.author: Tumezie
messages.text: one more; The pole mount has screw holes, the case has no place for an insert to fix to the case.
messages.content_html: one more; The pole mount has screw holes, the case has no place for an insert to fix to the case.

messages.author: Tumezie
messages.text: When you enable the GPS, the standoff sizes increase, which would allow for an insert to be added for the back plate (all my ideas compromise your very sleek small node), so apologies for suggestions that mess with its existing footprint (which I absolutely love). I don't mean to have any criticism because the existing design is perfect for its intended use; my use case is slightly different
messages.content_html: When you enable the GPS, the standoff sizes increase, which would allow for an insert to be added for the back plate (all my ideas compromise your very sleek small node), so apologies for suggestions that mess with its existing footprint (which I absolutely love). I don't mean to have any criticism because the existing design is perfect for its intended use; my use case is slightly different
